content.claim: Regional child-population trends in Hungary are spatially uneven: rural settlements in Western Transdanubia, Southern Baranya, and Northern Hungary experienced child-population contractions of 10-30% over a recent four-year period, while Northeastern counties (Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén, Szabolcs-Szatmár-Bereg, Heves) show high disadvantage concentrations (70-92% child disadvantage rates in some settlements). North-South and East-West regional divides are pronounced.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Regional analysis does not quantify whether child disadvantage concentration reflects demographic composition versus school-choice driven segregation
Data timeframe not specified; currency of regional statistics unclear
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
